taskDescription: Click on the key genetics terms related to patterns of inheritance in the text below.
textField:
In *autosomal* *dominant* inheritance, an affected *heterozygous* parent has a 50% chance of passing the *mutant* *allele* to each offspring. The trait shows *vertical* transmission through every generation. Examples include *Marfan* syndrome and *achondroplasia*.

*Autosomal* *recessive* disorders require two copies (*homozygous* state) of the mutant allele for expression. Both parents are typically *carriers* (*heterozygous*). *Consanguinity* increases the risk of these disorders. Examples include *cystic* *fibrosis* and *sickle* cell disease.

*X-linked* *recessive* disorders predominantly affect *males* because they have only one X *chromosome*. An affected father cannot transmit the condition to his *sons* but all *daughters* become *carriers*. *Anticipation* is the phenomenon of increasing severity in successive generations, seen in *trinucleotide* *repeat* expansion disorders like *myotonic* *dystrophy*. Variable *expressivity* and incomplete *penetrance* modify the clinical presentation.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! You identified many key terms.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of the terminology.
